I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following
interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise
specified.

 - input  clk
 - input  rst_n
 - input  valid_in
 - input  data_in (24 bits)
 - output reg valid_out
 - output reg data_out (128 bits)

The module should implement a data width conversion circuit that converts
24-bit input data into 128-bit output data. The circuit uses valid-only
handshake mechanism for both input and output interfaces.

The data width conversion should work as follows:
- Input interface: Uses valid-only handshake mechanism
  - valid_in indicates the validity of input data_in
- Output interface: Uses valid-only handshake mechanism
  - valid_out indicates the validity of output data_out

Data conversion process:
- The module receives 24-bit input data_in when valid_in is high
- The module accumulates multiple 24-bit inputs to form a 128-bit output
- The first received 24-bit data should be placed in the high bits of data_out
- The last received 24-bit data should be placed in the low bits of data_out
- After accumulating enough data to form a complete 128-bit word, the module
  outputs data_out with valid_out asserted

Based on the problem description above, the code below in TopModule has bug, please fix that:
```
module TopModule(
	input 				clk 		,   
	input 				rst_n		,
	input				valid_in	,
	input	[23:0]		data_in		,
 
 	output	reg			valid_out	,
	output  reg [127:0]	data_out
);
    reg [3:0]   cnt;
    reg [127:0] data_lock;
    
    always@(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(~rst_n)
            cnt <= 0;
        else
            cnt <= ~valid_in? cnt:cnt+1;
    end
    
    always@(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(~rst_n)
            valid_out <= 0;
        else
            valid_out <= (cnt==5 || cnt==10 || cnt==15) || valid_in;
    end
    
    always@(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(~rst_n)
            data_lock <= 0;
        else
            data_lock <= valid_in? {data_lock[103:0], data_in}: data_lock;
    end
    
    always@(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(~rst_n)
            data_out <= 0;
        else if(cnt==5)
            data_out <= valid_in? {data_lock[119:0], data_in[23:16]}: data_out;
        else if(cnt==10)
            data_out <= valid_in? {data_lock[111:0], data_in[23: 8]}: data_out;
        else if(cnt==15)
            data_out <= valid_in? {data_lock[103:0], data_in[23: 0]}: data_out;
        else
            data_out <= data_out;
    end
endmodule
```

======= VCD OUTPUT BEGIN =======
$date
	Mon Dec  1 21:25:18 2025
$end
$version
	Icarus Verilog
$end
$timescale
	1ps
$end
$scope module tb $end
$scope module stim1 $end
$var wire 1 ! clk $end
$upscope $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 1 " tb_mismatch $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var reg 1 ! clk $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 1 # rst_n $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 1 $ valid_in $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 24 % data_in [23:0] $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 1 & valid_out_ref $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 1 ' valid_out_dut $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 128 ( data_out_ref [127:0] $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 128 ) data_out_dut [127:0] $end
$upscope $end
$enddefinitions $end
$comment Show the parameter values. $end
$dumpall
$end

$dumpvars
